title: Package Design, Modules, Versions, Workspaces, and Dependency Provenance · Go Semantics Case: Module Version Selection
summary: A team must split a command and library, publish a stable module, test a two-module workspace, and review dependency changes. Planned artifact: Add and verify the go module version selection increment in a versioned multi-package module plan. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Explain semantic import versioning, minimal version selection, module graphs, sums, proxies, and private-module boundaries.
Interpret module, go, toolchain, require, replace, exclude, retract, and godebug directives without treating local overrides as releases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Module Version Selection · Theory Module Version Selection
steps.instruction:
theory case — A two-module workspace proposes dependency v3.2.0, a local replace, toolchain go1.26.5, and a public v2 package path. Explain MVS, checksum and proxy evidence, workspace-only success, API naming, and release compatibility.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Module Version Selection contract for the supplied data.
